arts.ttl: Odegaard makes honest admission as seven-goal Arsenal put scoring woes behind them
arts.sum: Martin Odegaard admits that he hasn’t contributed enough to the Arsenal attack this season after playing a starring role in their 7-1 thrashing of PSV Eindhoven on Tuesday night. Six different players got on the scoresheet for the Gunners, with the Norwegian scoring twice and assisting another to help the north London outfit put one […] The post Odegaard makes honest admission as seven-goal Arsenal put scoring woes behind them first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: PSV 1-7 Arsenal:Nwaneri and Lewis-Skelly make Champions League history
arts.sum: It was a famous night for Arsenal in the Champions League as they demolished PSV Eindhoven 7-1 in the Netherlands. Mikel Arteta’s men made history as they recorded their...

arts.ttl: Arsenal, Ethan Nwaneri, Myles Lewis-Skelly set new Champions League records in PSV obliteration
arts.sum: Arsenal, Ethan Nwaneri and Myles Lewis-Skelly all write their name into the Champions League record books in the Gunners' 7-1 destruction of PSV Eindhoven.

arts.ttl: PSV 1-7 Arsenal: Highlights, man of the match, stats as glorious Gunners make Champions League history
arts.sum: Arsenal put one foot firmly in the quarter-finals of the Champions League with a 6-1 annihilation of PSV Eindhoven in the first leg of their last-16 battle.

arts.ttl: Gunners smash Champions League record against PSV
arts.sum: The result it is our biggest away win in our European Cup and Champions League history, surpassing the famous 5-1 win against Inter Milan at the San Siro in 2003 as well as the drubbing of Sporting Lisbon back in November, which were our previous best efforts on the road in Europe's premier club competition. We have now become the first side in the tournament's history to score seven goals away from home in the knockout stages, and our rip-roaring start saw us score five times in the first 48 minutes of the game, the earliest an away side has ever hit that mark in the competition.

arts.ttl: Arsenal in seventh heaven in Champions League rout of PSV Eindhoven
arts.sum: Martin Odegaard scored twice in the Netherlands as Mikel Arteta’s side defied their lack of a recognised striker.

arts.ttl: Arsenal hit seven in thumping Champions League win away to PSV Eindhoven
arts.sum: Aston Villa and Real Madrid also hold first-leg leads.

arts.ttl: 'He knew': Odegaard shares how Lewis-Skelly reacted after coming off tonight and what Arsenal players now plan to do
arts.sum: Arsenal have one foot in the Champions League quarter-finals after running out 7-1 winners against PSV Eindhoven in their last-16 first leg in the Netherlands....

arts.ttl: The symbolic goal behind Arsenal’s ridiculous, record-breaking night
arts.sum: PSV 1-7 Arsenal: The Gunners will be heading to Madrid after recording the biggest away win in knockout history, helped by two teenagers from the Hale End academy

arts.ttl: ‘Listen’: Declan Rice has a point to make about Ethan Nwaneri after Arsenal’s 7-1 win over PSV
